title_en: Importance for Students and Available Editions
content_en:
Al-Isabah is essential reading for students of hadith sciences, Islamic history, and the biography of the Prophet's Companions. Students of hadith need it to verify the status of narrators at the Companion level, which is a prerequisite for evaluating the chains of transmission of specific hadith. Students of Islamic history need it as the most comprehensive and critically rigorous source for the lives of the generation who knew the Prophet personally.

The work is most productively used as a reference rather than read sequentially from beginning to end. Its alphabetical organization makes it easy to locate specific entries, and the detailed cross-referencing within entries helps students trace connections between Companions, identify family relationships, and follow the threads of transmission. The digital edition, with full-text search, greatly enhances the utility of the work for research.

For students interested in women's history in early Islam, the section on women Companions is particularly rewarding. It documents dozens of women who are known from the general historical literature but whose specific contributions as transmitters of hadith and as legal authorities have often been underappreciated. Al-Isabah makes visible the full extent of women's participation in the early Muslim community.

The standard printed edition is the eight-volume set produced by Dar al-Jil in Beirut, edited by Ali Muhammad al-Bajawi, which has been widely used in academic settings. A more recent critical edition was produced by Dar al-Kutub al-Ilmiyya in Beirut with improved commentary and indexing. The complete text is available in the major digital Islamic text repositories. Students should use al-Isabah alongside al-Isti'ab by Ibn Abd al-Barr and Usd al-Ghaba by Ibn al-Athir for the most comprehensive coverage of the Companion biographical literature. Each of the three major Companion biographical dictionaries has distinct strengths: al-Isti'ab for its depth on legal and hadith matters, Usd al-Ghaba for its narrative richness, and al-Isabah for its critical rigor and comprehensive scope. Ibn Hajar explicitly built on both earlier works while correcting their errors and adding entries they had missed, so al-Isabah represents the culmination of the tradition rather than merely one contribution to it. Students who read all three develop the most complete possible picture of the Companion generation.
content_id:
Al-Isabah adalah bacaan yang sangat penting bagi para penuntut ilmu hadits, sejarah Islam, dan biografi para Sahabat Nabi. Para penuntut ilmu hadits membutuhkannya untuk memverifikasi status para perawi di tingkat Sahabat, yang merupakan prasyarat dalam mengevaluasi sanad-sanad hadits tertentu. Para penuntut ilmu sejarah Islam membutuhkannya sebagai sumber paling komprehensif dan paling ketat secara kritis mengenai kehidupan generasi yang secara langsung mengenal Nabi.

Karya ini paling produktif digunakan sebagai rujukan, bukan dibaca secara berurutan dari awal hingga akhir. Susunannya yang alfabetis memudahkan pencarian entri tertentu, dan referensi silang yang terperinci di dalam setiap entri membantu para penuntut ilmu menelusuri hubungan antar Sahabat, mengidentifikasi relasi kekeluargaan, dan mengikuti jalur-jalur periwayatan. Edisi digital dengan fitur pencarian teks lengkap sangat meningkatkan kegunaan karya ini untuk keperluan penelitian.

Bagi para penuntut ilmu yang tertarik pada sejarah perempuan di masa awal Islam, bagian mengenai para Sahabiyah sangat berharga. Bagian ini mendokumentasikan puluhan perempuan yang dikenal dari literatur sejarah umum, namun kontribusi spesifik mereka sebagai periwayat hadits dan sebagai otoritas hukum kerap kali kurang dihargai. Al-Isabah menampakkan secara penuh sejauh mana partisipasi perempuan dalam komunitas Muslim awal.

Edisi cetak standar adalah set delapan jilid yang diterbitkan oleh Dar al-Jil di Beirut, disunting oleh Ali Muhammad al-Bajawi, yang telah banyak digunakan di lingkungan akademis. Edisi kritis yang lebih baru diterbitkan oleh Dar al-Kutub al-Ilmiyya di Beirut dengan komentar dan indeks yang lebih baik. Teks lengkapnya tersedia dalam repositori teks Islam digital utama. Para penuntut ilmu sebaiknya menggunakan al-Isabah bersama al-Isti'ab karya Ibn Abd al-Barr dan Usd al-Ghaba karya Ibn al-Atsir untuk mendapatkan cakupan paling komprehensif atas literatur biografi Sahabat. Masing-masing dari ketiga kamus biografi Sahabat utama ini memiliki keunggulan tersendiri: al-Isti'ab dalam kedalaman pembahasan masalah hukum dan hadits, Usd al-Ghaba dalam kekayaan narasi, dan al-Isabah dalam ketelitian kritis serta cakupannya yang menyeluruh. Ibn Hajar secara eksplisit membangun karyanya di atas kedua karya pendahulunya sekaligus mengoreksi kesalahan-kesalahan mereka dan menambahkan entri-entri yang terlewatkan, sehingga al-Isabah merupakan puncak dari tradisi ini, bukan sekadar satu kontribusi di dalamnya. Para penuntut ilmu yang membaca ketiga karya tersebut akan mendapatkan gambaran sepaling lengkap mengenai generasi para Sahabat.
title_id: Pentingnya bagi Para Pelajar dan Edisi-Edisi yang Tersedia
content_ar: Al-Isabah is الجوهري reading for students of الحديث sciences, Islamic history, and the biography of النبي ﷺ's الصحابة. Students of الحديث need it to verify the status of narrators at the Companion level, which is a prerequisite for evaluating the chains of transmission of specific الحديث. Students of Islamic history need it as the most comprehensive and critically rigorous source for the lives of the generation who knew النبي ﷺ personally. The work is most productively used as a reference rather than read sequentially from beginning to end. Its alphabetical organization makes it easy to locate specific entries, and the detailed cross-referencing within entries helps students trace connections between الصحابة, identify الأسرة relationships, and follow the threads of transmission. The digital edition, with full-text search, greatly enhances the utility of the work for research. For students interested in women's history in early الإسلام, the الفصل on women الصحابة is particularly rewarding. It documents dozens of women who are known from the general historical literature but whose specific contributions as transmitters of الحديث and as legal authorities have often been underappreciated. Al-Isabah makes visible the full extent of women's participation in the early مسلم الجماعة. The standard printed edition is the eight-volume set produced by Dar al-Jil in Beirut, edited by علي محمد ﷺ al-Bajawi, which has been widely used in الأكاديمي settings. A more recent البالغ الأهمية edition was produced by Dar al-Kutub al-Ilmiyya in Beirut with improved commentary and indexing. The complete text is available in the major digital Islamic text repositories. Students should use al-Isabah alongside al-Isti'ab by Ibn Abd al-Barr and Usd al-Ghaba by Ibn al-Athir for the most comprehensive coverage of the Companion biographical literature. Each of the three major Companion biographical dictionaries has distinct strengths: al-Isti'ab for its depth on legal and الحديث matters, Usd al-Ghaba for its narrative richness, and al-Isabah for its البالغ الأهمية rigor and comprehensive scope. Ibn Hajar explicitly built on both earlier works بينما correcting their errors and adding entries they had missed, لذا al-Isabah represents the culmination of the التراث rather than merely one contribution to it. Students who read all three develop the most complete possible picture of the Companion generation.
